title: I am using Monday to create a training schedule, and I can't figure out how to count up host/co-host totals from subitems.
excerpt:
I use Monday to create a schedule of live trainings and workshops. The training is the item; the sub-items are each event date and who is hosting/co-hosting.

posts.author: emilypshea (Emily)
posts.cookedHtml:
I use Monday to create a schedule of live trainings and workshops. The training is the item; the sub-items are each event date and who is hosting/co-hosting.

I want to be able to create a table that tracks how many trainings people are hosting and co-hosting. Is this possible?

Screenshot 2024-11-15 at 10.02.52 AM2028×392 102 KB

I would really appreciate any help in how to format this!

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Emily, this may be suitable if you don’t have too many people.

  1. Set up a numbers widget
  2. Include items and sub-items
  3. Create filters for a singular person on Owner OR Co Host
  4. Duplicate the chart, and change the filters for each person

image1851×471 40.5 KB

Alternatives that might work would be Workload Widget (for day, week, month selection), or a Pivot Table (Enterprise tier, or an App to slice the data). Let me know if that doesn’t work for you. You can book in with me here if you prefer a live call to work through this. https://calendar.google.com/calendar/u/0/r?tab=mc
